events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. Key conclusion: pad wear indicator contact signals the friction material is at end-of-life, so the correct sequence requires pad (and rotor inspection/replacement) work to happen *before* caliper/wheel reassembly — reassembling over worn pads is a sequence violation and an installed-defective condition, so the job fails and must stop until pads are replaced and the proper order (inspect → replace → torque → seat → verify) is followed.
